simple_types.name: Temperature
simple_types.code: type Temperature is new Float range 0.0 .. 1.0;

documentation.description: Probability of keeping a new solution

subprograms.name: Cyclic_Cooling

documentation.description:
T := ((Clock - Start) / Period) ^ Power
Note that Start is reset if Clock - Start > Period

subprograms.name: Lineal_Cooling

documentation.description:
(T := T - 1/Steps).
If cyclic, Temp goes up to 1.0 if 0.0 is reached

subprograms.name: Normalize_Greedy
subprograms.code:
function Normalize_Greedy
  (Old_Cost,
   New_Cost : in Cost;
   Temp     : in Temperature) return Acceptability

documentation.description: New < Old strictly. Not really simulated annealing...

subprograms.name: Normalize_Kirkpatrick
subprograms.code:
function Normalize_Kirkpatrick
  (Old_Cost,
   New_Cost : in Cost;
   Temp     : in Temperature) return Acceptability

documentation.description:
Classical function from the original paper by Kirkpatrick et al.
Actually adapted since T is [0..1] instead of original [0..inf]

subprograms.name: Proportional_Cooling

packages.name: Manual_Cooling

documentation.description:
Expected package usage is to manually divide temperature when you need
it.
In this way you can keep low temperatures for as long as necessary.
Or, using Update, it will be done for you
